title: The Sonnets XXIV - Mine eye hath playï؟½d the painter and hath stellï؟½d
content:
آ آ آ آ Mine eye hath playï؟½d the painter and hath stellï؟½d,
آ آ آ آ Thy beautyï؟½s form in table of my heart;
آ آ آ آ My body is the frame wherein ï؟½tis held,
آ آ آ آ And perspective it is best painterï؟½s art.
آ آ آ آ For through the painter must you see his skill,
آ آ آ آ To find where your true image picturï؟½d lies,
آ آ آ آ Which in my bosomï؟½s shop is hanging still,
آ آ آ آ That hath his windows glazed with thine eyes.
آ آ آ آ Now see what good turns eyes for eyes have done:
آ آ آ آ Mine eyes have drawn thy shape, and thine for me
آ آ آ آ Are windows to my breast, where-through the sun
آ آ آ آ Delights to peep, to gaze therein on thee;
آ آ آ آ Yet eyes this cunning want to grace their art,
آ آ آ آ They draw but what they see, know not the heart.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Shakespeare delivers a powerful performance in "The Sonnets XXIV - Mine eye hath playï؟½d the painter and hath stellï؟½d"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
